message:
fix(fcp-registry): enforce real TUF/Sigstore verification (br-pcmm8)

Closes flywheel_connectors-pcmm8 (P2/REVIEW/MEDIUM).

enforce_supply_chain_verification_config previously treated require_tuf
and require_sigstore as satisfied whenever ANY SupplyChainEvidence was
passed — including a default-constructed one with no verifier ever
having run. Operators could deploy with require_tuf=true believing they
were protected while a trivial placeholder satisfied the gate.

Fix: SupplyChainEvidence gains two new booleans — tuf_verified and
sigstore_verified — that are populated ONLY by real verifier adapters.
enforce_supply_chain_verification_config now requires those bools to be
true, not merely that evidence is Some.

fwc production sites (build_registry_supply_chain_evidence in
supply_chain_cmd.rs, RegistrySupplyChainEvidence in main.rs) hold both
booleans at false with explicit SECURITY comments — those paths only
validate publisher signatures, not TUF/Sigstore. Deployments that set
require_tuf/require_sigstore will now correctly fail there until real
adapter wiring lands, matching the reviewer's intended fail-closed
semantics.

Added regression tests covering the bypass:
- tests::verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_tuf_evidence
- tests::verify_bundle_rejects_unverified_sigstore_evidence
Each asserts that evidence-present-but-unverified is rejected, AND that
flipping the boolean to true satisfies the gate (positive control).

All 34 SupplyChainEvidence struct-literal construction sites (lib.rs,
no_mock_integration.rs, fwc main/supply_chain_cmd) updated to carry
both new fields — compile-driven, not regex-driven.

Verified:
- cargo check -p fcp-registry -p fwc --all-targets: clean
- cargo test -p fcp-registry --lib: 354 pass, 6 pre-existing fail
  (unrelated: mock_registry_* and metamorphic_install_verify hit
  ObjectStore::AlreadyExists; manifest_signing_bytes_different_manifests_differ)
- cargo test -p fcp-registry --features test-mocks --test no_mock_integration:
  49 pass, 39 pre-existing fail (unrelated: manifest validation rule for
  operation capability declaration). Confirmed baseline-equivalent via
  stash-and-rerun.

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose

files.patch:
@@ -549,6 +549,8 @@ min_slsa_level = 3
     // Evidence with SLSA level 1 (below required 3)
     let evidence = SupplyChainEvidence {
         transparency_log_present: false,
+        tuf_verified: false,
+        sigstore_verified: false,
         attestations: vec![AttestationEvidence {
             attestation_type: AttestationType::InToto,
             slsa_level: Some(1),
@@ -599,6 +601,8 @@ trusted_builders = ["github-actions"]

             attestation_type: AttestationType::InToto,
             slsa_level: None,

@@ -14757,8 +14757,13 @@ fn verify_registry_attestation(
         .verify(&signing_bytes, &signature)
         .context("registry attestation signature verification failed")?;
 
+    // SECURITY: tuf_verified / sigstore_verified stay false — this path
+    // only validates the publisher signature, not TUF or Sigstore
+    // attestations (br-pcmm8).
     Ok(RegistrySupplyChainEvidence {
         transparency_log_present: false,
+        tuf_verified: false,
+        sigstore_verified: false,
         attestations: vec![RegistryAttestationEvidence {
             attestation_type: fcp_manifest::AttestationType::InToto,
             slsa_level: Some(attestation.slsa_level),

@@ -649,8 +649,16 @@ fn build_attestation(
 fn build_registry_supply_chain_evidence(
     attestation: &SupplyChainAttestation,
 ) -> SupplyChainEvidence {
+    // SECURITY: tuf_verified / sigstore_verified stay false here — this
+    // function synthesizes evidence from a SupplyChainAttestation parsed
+    // from CLI input, not from real TUF or Sigstore verifier adapters.
+    // Deployments that set require_tuf / require_sigstore will correctly
+    // fail until an adapter wires those booleans to real attestations
+    // (br-pcmm8).
     SupplyChainEvidence {
         transparency_log_present: false,
+        tuf_verified: false,
+        sigstore_verified: false,
         attestations: vec![AttestationEvidence {
             attestation_type: AttestationType::InToto,
             slsa_level: Some(attestation.slsa_level),
